title: rubus × corymbiflorus
intro: Rubus × corymbiflorus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species within the Rosaceae family, resulting from the cross between the red raspberry and the black raspberry. The thimbleberry is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as acting as an antimicrobial agent to prevent infections. Thimbleberry can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried powder, and it is often used in herbal remedies to support overall well-being.
